mnemonics.title: P.P.H.C. - Public People Have Choices
mnemonics.content: P = Public (Open to everyone, like public park), P = Private (Exclusive, like private club), H = Hybrid (Best of both, like hybrid cars), C = Community (Shared by specific group, like community garden)

mnemonics.title: SCALE for Cloud Types
mnemonics.content: S - Shared resources (Public), C - Controlled access (Private), A - Aggregated resources (Hybrid), L - Limited users (Community), E - Elastic scaling (all cloud types)

mnemonics.title: COST Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: C - Cost-effective (Public), O - On-premises (Private), S - Scalable (Hybrid), T - Tenant-specific (Community)

acronyms.expansion: Cloud services offered to the general public over the internet
acronyms.usage: When describing shared, multi-tenant infrastructure available to anyone (e.g., AWS, Azure public cloud)

acronyms.expansion: Cloud infrastructure dedicated to a single organization
acronyms.usage: When discussing exclusive, on-premises or dedicated cloud resources for enterprise security

acronyms.expansion: Combination of public and private cloud infrastructure working together
acronyms.usage: When explaining mixed environments that leverage both in-house and external cloud services

acronyms.expansion: Cloud infrastructure shared among several organizations with common requirements
acronyms.usage: When describing shared clouds for specific industries or communities (e.g., healthcare, government)
When referring to cloud infrastructure provisioning (servers, storage, networking)
When describing cloud-hosted applications accessible over the internet

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a public library - anyone can access shared resources (books, WiFi) but the building and infrastructure are owned/managed by the government. It's open, pay-per-use, and scalable.
Think of your own bedroom - it's exclusively yours, you control who enters, customize it your way, and it's for your personal use only. No one else can access it without permission.
Picture a smart home system - you have private infrastructure (your home security) connected to public services (electricity, water supply from municipal corporations). They work together seamlessly.
Imagine a residential welfare association (RWA) colony - common areas and facilities are shared among only those who live in that specific society, not open to outsiders.

Draw a table with 4 columns (Public, Private, Hybrid, Community) and rows showing Ownership, Security Level, Cost, Users, and Access. Use colored boxes - Green (High), Yellow (Medium), Red (Low) for quick visual recall.

analogies.analogy: Like a public bus or metro - anyone can board, pay their fare, and travel. The government/operator manages it, and you share space with strangers. Cost-effective and readily available.
Like your personal car - you own it, maintain it, control who rides in it, and it's exclusively for your use. Expensive but offers maximum privacy and customization.
Like using both personal vehicle and public transport - you drive your car for private/urgent work and take the metro for regular commute. You get benefits of both worlds.
Like a housing society clubhouse - it's shared only by society members, maintained collectively, and used for common activities. Not open to public but not private to one person.
